does: This paper documents OpenAI's collaborative efforts with the US Center for AI Safety and Innovation and the UK AI Safety Institute to advance safety and security practices in AI system development. The work reports on joint initiatives designed to strengthen technical safeguards, governance frameworks, and security protocols across AI research and deployment. It presents concrete progress on partnerships that bring together government agencies and industry stakeholders to identify vulnerabilities, establish best practices, and coordinate on reducing risks associated with advanced AI systems.
connects: This work builds on the established tradition of multi-stakeholder coordination on emerging technology risks, extending prior efforts in AI governance and safety research conducted by individual labs and academic institutions. It responds to growing recognition that safety challenges require coordination between industry, government, and international bodies, building on frameworks developed through earlier NIST AI Risk Management initiatives and discussions around responsible AI deployment. The partnership model reflects lessons from prior collaborative safety work while establishing more formal structures for sustained information sharing and coordinated problem-solving.
enables: This collaborative infrastructure enables future development of shared safety standards, interoperable security protocols, and coordinated incident response mechanisms across the AI research and deployment landscape. The partnership creates channels for rapid dissemination of safety findings and early warning systems for emerging risks. By establishing formal coordination structures between government agencies and private AI developers, this work enables more systematic identification of safety gaps, development of regulatory approaches grounded in technical reality, and harmonization of security practices across jurisdictions and organizations working on advanced AI systems.
